name: Tapenade
intro: I often make this tapanata. When made with green olives rather than black, it tastes quite different. Black ones are the ripe olives, so they have a softer and rounder taste. Green ones, not so ripe and harder, have a little more bite to them. It’s a matter of taste, so try both and see which you prefer.

ingredients.item: good-quality black or green olives, pitted
toasted bread or crackers, to serve

method: Place the olives, garlic, anchovy fillets, capers, chilli and basil in a food processor and blend, adding the oil slowly in a fine stream until you have the desired consistency.
Taste and season with salt and pepper. Serve with crunchy bread or crackers.

The tapanata keeps very well in the fridge for at least one week. Just pour it into a clean jar, bang the jar on the table to make sure there are no air pockets and seal the tapanata with a thin layer of virgin olive oil.
